---
title: "Advanced Graph Patterns"
subtitle: "A list of advanced CIDOC CRM graph patterns"
---

This page lists some advanced graph patterns that we encounter when modelling data in [LINCS](https://lincsproject.ca) using the [CIDOC CRM](https://cidoc-crm.org) ontology. This are more difficult to use compared to [basic graph patterns](./basic-graph-patterns.html), but offer a more detailed and comprehensive modelling.

## General Patterns

### Attribute Assignment

An attribute is assigned to something by someone. Used to model uncertain or conflicting statements.


## Patterns Involving People

### Identity (Gender, Ethnicity, Religion, ...)

A person has a certain identity. In LINCS, identities are always attributed by someone using the attribute assignment pattern.

[DIAGRAM]

## Patterns Involving Places

The following patterns are applicable to any event or its subclasses colored in blue, such as Activity (including occupations), Birth, Death, Production, Move, etc.

### Presence

A presence is used to model the extent in space and time of a certain phenomenon, such as the location of an object over time, the spatiotemporal extent of an event, etc.

[DIAGRAMS]
